# Open questions

Recorded here rather than left implicit, because each one would otherwise
surface at review.

## The exclusion filter

The source query drops rows where `datemax` is one of
260, 120, 150. Nobody involved in the
export knows why. Because the filter applies across all findspots rather
than to selected ones, it shifts every derived figure to some unknown
degree. It is exported as `lado:excludedDatemax` so that the selection is
at least visible.

## Findspot identity

Findspots have no key in the database, only a text label, so URIs are
derived from that text. Correcting a label breaks the URI. The data
currently contain *Böckleareal (descruction layer period II)* — a typo for
*destruction* — in exactly such a field. Either it is corrected before
publication or it is left permanently untouched; both are defensible, but
the decision cannot be deferred past the first publication of the URIs.

## Era convention

Confirmed as historical: −40 in the database means 40 BC, and the derived
`xsd:gYear` is shifted by one because XSD counts astronomically, so 40 BC
becomes `-0039`. Only the calendar label is shifted. The decimal
`time:numericPosition` keeps the source value on a documented local scale,
`samian:trs_ips_year`, because shifting only negative values would tear
the scale apart at zero and would not be reversible.

One consequence worth flagging: interval ends near the era boundary can
produce `"0000"^^xsd:gYear`. That is astronomically 1 BC and valid under
XSD 1.1 but not under XSD 1.0. A validator working to the older
specification will object, and the affected literal is the rounded
calendar label rather than the authoritative numeric position.

## Defects in the published discovery-site data (2019 release)

The published file dates from 2019 and is due for regeneration. Three
defects should be addressed when that happens; none was introduced here.

- `prov:` is bound to `http://www.w3.org/ns/prov-o/`, so all six
  provenance predicates in that file denote nothing.
- Pleiades identifiers carry a trailing `.0` inside the URI, leaving all
  280 links unreachable. The `.0` originates in the database column, which
  is why this export casts to integer.
- `foaf:homepage` holds a string containing angle brackets rather than a
  URI.

## The edge quality measures

`lado:qStart` and `lado:qEnd` are distorted by proximity to the calendar
origin. They are exported because they drive whisker colour in the
published figure and removing them would break comparability with the web
output, but they should not be presented as quality measures without that
caveat. `lado:qInterval` is unaffected and is the measure to prefer.
